mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate crossing above 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration that immediately triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, or the signal loses predictive power after 2015.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth that drives corporate earnings expectations higher, producing positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or housing data decouples from equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reverses to negative territory during a tightening cycle, signaling supply-side contraction that forces equity repricing and generates negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day equity returns in over 60% of cases, invalidating the supply contraction channel.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or zero Rank IC for 5-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows since 2000.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation spikes above 0.4 percent force central banks to accelerate tightening expectations, compressing equity multiples and driving negative quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally by more than 2% over 60 days following CPIAUCSL monthly changes above 0.4% in over half of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer spending relative to output, triggering inventory corrections and negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence metric produces a Rank IC below 0.03 for 20-day returns, or retail sales outpace GDP without equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production accelerates above its 6-month moving average during a post-shock recovery, signaling rapid capacity utilization that boosts equity valuations within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration during recovery phases fails to generate positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of observed instances, or the signal is statistically indistinguishable from noise.
